name: Flipkart Product Catalog — Advanced JPA Domain Model
scenario: Build the Flipkart product catalog domain model with all the advanced JPA relationship patterns: Product ↔ Category @ManyToMany with correct cascades, @Embeddable value objects (WarehouseAddress, Price), @ElementCollection for tags and specifications, soft delete, and auditing.
description: Complex JPA domain: Category (with self-reference), Product (@ManyToMany, @Embedded, @ElementCollection, soft delete, auditing), Seller (@ManyToOne from Product). @DataJpaTest verifying all relationship behaviors.

core.description: @Embeddable WarehouseAddress (city, pincode, state). @Embeddable Price (amount BigDecimal, currency String default 'INR'). Both with proper @Column annotations.
Category @Entity: id, name UNIQUE, description, @ManyToOne Category parent (self-reference, nullable). @ManyToMany(mappedBy='categories') Set<Product> products.

Product @Entity with: @ManyToMany Set<Category> (cascade={PERSIST,MERGE}, @JoinTable), @Embedded WarehouseAddress warehouseLocation, @Embedded Price price (@AttributeOverrides if needed), @ElementCollection Set<String> tags, @ElementCollection Set<String> imageUrls, boolean deleted=false, @CreatedDate/@LastModifiedDate (optional).
Soft delete: @SQLDelete on Product (UPDATE SET deleted=true), @Where(clause='deleted=false'). softDelete() method. ALL JPA queries automatically filter deleted products.

ProductService (@Service @Transactional): addProduct(name, price, warehouseCity, Set<String> tags), addToCategory(productId, categoryId), removeFromCategory(productId, categoryId), softDeleteProduct(id). Uses helper methods for bidirectional consistency.

@DataJpaTest (5 tests): (1) addToCategory links both sides, (2) removeFromCategory doesn't delete category, (3) soft delete hides from findAll, (4) tags saved and loaded correctly, (5) parent category self-reference works. All 5 pass.
Flyway migration V6: CREATE TABLE categories, CREATE TABLE products, CREATE TABLE product_categories, CREATE TABLE product_tags, CREATE TABLE product_image_urls. All with appropriate constraints.

bonus.description: Add @EntityListeners(AuditingEntityListener.class) + @CreatedDate + @LastModifiedDate + @CreatedBy + @LastModifiedBy to Product. @EnableJpaAuditing + AuditorAware<String> @Bean. @DataJpaTest verifying createdAt set on save.

Replace Product ↔ Category @ManyToMany with explicit ProductCategory @Entity when needing a 'featured' boolean and 'displayOrder' int in the join table. ProductCategoryService.featureProduct(productId, categoryId).

Add Seller @Entity (id, businessName, gstin, @Embedded sellerAddress). Product has @ManyToOne Seller (lazy). ProductRepository with JOIN FETCH Seller to avoid N+1. @DataJpaTest asserting exactly 1 query for findAllWithSeller.

data_to_use:
Category hierarchy:
  Electronics (root)
  └── Mobiles (parent=Electronics)
      └── 5G Phones (parent=Mobiles)
  Appliances (root)

Products:
  iPhone 15 Pro: ₹89,999, warehouse=Mumbai
    → categories: Electronics, Mobiles, 5G Phones
    → tags: 5G, iOS, Face ID
  Samsung Galaxy S24: ₹74,999, warehouse=Delhi
    → categories: Electronics, Mobiles
    → tags: 5G, Android

Soft delete test: delete iPhone15, findAll returns only Samsung
example_output:
# Hibernate DDL (H2, create-drop in tests):
create table categories (id varchar(36) primary key, name varchar(200) not null unique, parent_id varchar(36), ...)
create table products (id varchar(36) primary key, name varchar(200) not null, deleted boolean not null default false, wh_city varchar(100), ...)
create table product_categories (product_id varchar(36), category_id varchar(36))
create table product_tags (product_id varchar(36), tag varchar(255))

# Tests:
✓ addToCategory_linksBothSides
✓ removeFromCategory_categoryNotDeleted
✓ softDelete_hiddenFromFindAll
✓ tags_savedAndLoaded
✓ selfReference_parentCategoryWorks
ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. @ManyToMany cascade: NEVER include REMOVE or ALL — only {PERSIST, MERGE}. @SQLDelete: 'UPDATE products SET deleted=true WHERE id=?' — use actual table name, not entity name. @Where: 'deleted=false' — applied to ALL JPA queries automatically. Helper methods: always update BOTH sides (product.categories.add(cat) AND cat.products.add(product)). Self-referential: @ManyToOne @JoinColumn(name='parent_id') Category parent — nullable for root categories.
submission_instructions: GitHub push. All 5 @DataJpaTest tests pass. V6 Flyway migration SQL file included. README explains cascade type choices and why CascadeType.REMOVE is excluded from @ManyToMany.
tips_for_success: Cascade rule: if sharing → {PERSIST,MERGE}; if exclusive → ALL + orphanRemoval
@SQLDelete uses SQL table name (products), not JPQL entity name (Product)
@Where applies to ALL queries — findAll(), findById(), derived queries
testEm.flush() + testEm.clear() before assertions: forces DB write and clears cache
concepts_reinforced: @Embeddable value objects stored as columns in parent table
